key_insight: The paper accelerates phase-field cohesive zone modeling to predict mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue in UHPC and high-strength steel using load-envelope cycling and adaptive fatigue degradation.
engineering_relevance: It offers a more practical simulation route for designing cyclic-loaded infrastructure with high-performance materials, reducing reliance on costly and time-consuming fatigue testing.
method: Derives a generic stress-based failure criterion for UHPC from biaxial tensile tests.
Integrates a fatigue degradation function into the PF-CZM framework.
Uses an acceleration algorithm with envelope loading and adaptive cyclic increments to simulate three fatigue stages efficiently.
evidence_signal: The model is validated against relevant experimental comparisons and is reported to capture both mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue behavior of UHPC and HSS.
watch_item: Because the acceleration relies on envelope-load approximation and adaptive cycle stepping, accuracy may depend on how well these simplifications represent the true loading history.
paper_title: High performance construction materials fracture and high cycle fatigue assessment based on accelerated PF-CZM
